name: Midtown Gold and Silver Buyers
description_short: Dallas Gold & Silver Exchange buys and sells precious metals, jewelry, watches, and bullion across 6 North Texas locations with over 40 years of experience.
description_long: Dallas Gold & Silver Exchange (DGSE) has operated for more than four decades as a buyer and seller of precious metals and luxury goods in North Texas. The company operates six locations across the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex, including flagship stores in Dallas, Euless, Frisco, Grapevine, Grand Prairie, and Lewisville, with an additional Fort Worth location announced as coming soon. DGSE positions itself as a premium alternative to typical pawn shops, emphasizing long-term customer relationships and professional service. The company's core business model centers on purchasing gold and silver from consumers—whether large quantities or small pieces of broken jewelry—while also retailing coins, bullion, fine jewelry, engagement rings, and luxury watches. DGSE distinguishes itself through multiple service offerings beyond standard buying and selling. The company maintains a state-of-the-art jewelry and watch repair facility with in-house craftsmen capable of restoration and custom piece creation. Their retail inventory includes overstock and closeout merchandise from luxury retailers, featuring designer brands such as Tiffany & Co. and Cartier, allowing them to offer discounted fine jewelry alongside their precious metals business. This hybrid model—combining cash-for-gold buying with upscale jewelry retail—creates a unique positioning that differs from traditional pawn shops. For consumers, DGSE offers genuine utility for liquidating precious metals and jewelry while providing legitimate retail options for purchasing fine goods at discounted prices. The primary caveat is that this remains a for-profit business focused on precious metals and luxury items; customers should have realistic expectations about buyback prices and understand that retail jewelry markups apply even on discounted designer goods. The company's longevity and multi-location presence suggest operational stability, though individual transaction terms will vary by item and market conditions.
pros: 40+ year operating history with established brand reputation in North Texas
Six physical locations enable convenient local access across Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex
In-house jewelry and watch repair services with state-of-the-art facility for restoration and custom work
Discounted designer jewelry inventory from luxury retailers (Tiffany & Co., Cartier overstocks)
Buys all quantities of gold and silver, including broken jewelry and small pieces
Offers full transparency and stated commitment to integrity in precious metals transactions
Services span both buying (cash for metals) and selling (coins, bullion, fine jewelry, watches)
cons: Buyback prices for gold and silver will be below spot market value due to business model
Designer jewelry purchases, while discounted, still carry retail markups above wholesale cost
Limited geographic reach restricted to North Texas; not a nationwide option
No online purchasing or remote valuation mentioned; requires in-person store visits
Repair and custom services likely carry premium pricing typical of jewelry industry
best_for: North Texas residents seeking to liquidate gold, silver, or jewelry for immediate cash
Consumers interested in purchasing discounted fine jewelry and luxury watches locally
Watch and jewelry owners needing professional repair or restoration services
Bullion investors in the Dallas-Fort Worth area buying or selling precious metals
diagnosis: DGSE is best for North Texas residents needing to sell precious metals and jewelry for cash or seeking discounted fine jewelry and watches in person. The main caveat is that buyback prices will reflect business margins below spot market rates, and all services require visiting a physical location in their service area.
services: Gold buying and cash payment for precious metals
Silver buying including broken jewelry and scrap pieces
Bullion sales (coins and bars) with stated transparency
Fine jewelry retail including engagement rings and designer pieces
Designer jewelry liquidation (overstock and closeout inventory)
